issues.title: Coverage: Users: user attachments
issues.body_raw:
**Domain:** Users
**Priority:** High
**Effort:** M (1–3 days)

## Background
Add coverage for user attachments (the `Attachments` resource hanging off a
user, used for ID scans, deposit receipts, ILL paperwork, etc.). The wrapper
has no methods for this resource today.

The audit (2026-05-01) flagged two ambiguities in the original proposal that
need to be resolved during implementation, not assumed away:

1. The `GET /attachments/{attachment_id}` shape — the docs describe an
   `Attachment` object with `expand=content` / `content_no_encoding` query
   params, **not** a raw-bytes binary download as the original signature
   implied (`-> bytes`).
2. The `POST` shape — the original notes called it "multipart"; the docs
   describe an Attachment object payload with the file content embedded
   (base64 inside the Attachment object). Multipart vs JSON+base64 must be
   verified against the developer-network docs before coding.

## Relevant Alma API documentation
- Alma dev-network: https://developers.exlibrisgroup.com/alma/apis/users/
- Endpoints touched:
  - `GET /almaws/v1/users/{user_id}/attachments`
  - `POST /almaws/v1/users/{user_id}/attachments`
  - `GET /almaws/v1/users/{user_id}/attachments/{attachment_id}`
- Note: docs describe an Attachment XML/JSON object; the GET single-attachment
  endpoint accepts an `expand` query param taking values `content` (returns
  base64-encoded content inline) or `content_no_encoding` (returns raw text
  inline) — see "User Attachments" page on the dev network.
- No DELETE endpoint is documented for user attachments.

## Current wrapper behavior
None. `BibliographicRecords.create_representation` /
`link_file_to_representation` (`src/almaapitk/domains/bibs.py:569-690`) provide
a precedent for binary uploads on a different domain; reuse the helper
patterns there only after confirming the user-attachments endpoint actually
accepts the same shape.

## Expected wrapper behavior

```python
def list_user_attachments(self, user_id: str) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]: ...

def get_user_attachment(
    self,
    user_id: str,
    attachment_id: str,
    expand: str = None,    # "content" or "content_no_encoding"
) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    """
    Returns the Attachment object. When `expand` is set, the object contains
    the file payload inline (base64 if 'content', plaintext if
    'content_no_encoding'). Callers needing raw bytes should base64-decode
    the inline content themselves.
    """

def upload_user_attachment(
    self,
    user_id: str,
    file_path: str,
    attachment_data: Dict[str, Any] = None,
) -> AlmaResponse:
    """
    Uploads a file to a user's attachments. Wraps the file bytes into the
    Attachment object format documented by Alma (verify exact shape against
    dev-network docs at implementation time — multipart vs JSON+base64 is
    NOT to be guessed).
    """
```

## Implementation notes
- **Verify before coding** whether the POST endpoint expects multipart form
  data or a JSON body with embedded base64 content. The original ticket
  asserted multipart; the audit flagged this as unverified. Resolve by
  reading the developer-network "Create Attachment" page directly.
- `get_user_attachment` returns the Attachment object as a dict; do not
  silently base64-decode — let the caller decide.
- `attachment_type` was on the original signature; replace with a generic
  `attachment_data: Dict[str, Any]` so callers can supply the full
  documented Attachment object (note, type, etc.).
- Validate `user_id`, `attachment_id`, and `file_path` (must exist on disk)
  at method entry.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] All three endpoints exposed with type hints and Google-style docstrings.
- [ ] `get_user_attachment` accepts the documented `expand` query parameter
      and returns the Attachment dict (NOT raw `bytes`).
- [ ] `upload_user_attachment` body shape matches what the developer-network
      docs actually require (verified at implementation time, not guessed).
- [ ] Unit tests (mocked HTTP) cover: list, get without expand, get with
      `expand=content`, upload happy path, upload with missing file.
- [ ] At least one SANDBOX integration test that round-trips an attachment
      (upload → list → get → verify content).
- [ ] No DELETE method is implemented (Alma does not document one).
- [ ] No `print()` — all operations logged via `alma_logging`. File contents
      are NOT logged.
- [ ] `scripts/smoke_import.py` and `tests/test_public_api_contract.py` pass.

## Open questions
- Multipart vs JSON+base64 for upload — must be verified against the
  developer-network "Create Attachment" page before implementation.
- Whether `expand=content` is mandatory or merely recommended for
  `get_user_attachment` callers needing the file body.

## Audit notes (2026-05-01)
- Primary audit: **Partially aligned**. Endpoints correct; the
  raw-bytes return type and multipart upload assumption are not confirmed by
  docs. Action: verify response shape and request shape during implementation.
- Codex direct audit: **Partially aligned** — same finding, with the explicit
  doc reference to Attachment objects + `expand=content` /
  `content_no_encoding`.
- Decision: re-shape the signature to return `Dict[str, Any]` and accept
  `expand`, and replace ad-hoc multipart with a doc-verified upload body.

## Files to touch
- `src/almaapitk/domains/users.py` (extend existing `Users` class)
- `tests/unit/domains/test_users.py`

## References
- Alma dev-network: https://developers.exlibrisgroup.com/alma/apis/users/
- Use the `alma-api-expert` skill for endpoint quirks and validation rules

<!-- prereqs:auto:begin -->
## Prerequisites

**Recommended (would simplify or improve this work, but not strictly blocking):**
- #3 — Persistent requests.Session (every new HTTP call inherits connection pooling)
- #4 — Consolidate HTTP verbs into _request() (every new method routes through one chokepoint)
- #5 — Retry with exponential backoff for 429 / 5xx (every new method gets 429/5xx resilience for free)
- #9 — Map Alma error codes to specific exception subclasses (new methods raise typed Alma errors instead of generic AlmaAPIError)
- #10 — Propagate tracking_id and alma_code on errors (errors carry tracking_id for support escalation)
- #14 — Replace print() with logger; remove safe_request() (no print() statements in the new methods)
- #11 — iter_paged() generator at the client level (list/search methods should use iter_paged() instead of manual offset loops)

<!-- prereqs:auto:end -->

## Notes for the implementing agent
- Mirror an existing domain method as the pattern source (e.g., `Acquisitions.get_invoice` for read, `Acquisitions.create_invoice_simple` for write).
- All public methods must have type hints + Google-style docstrings.
- Validate required inputs at top of method and raise `AlmaValidationError` with a clear message.
- Log method entry with key parameters (info), success with result identifiers (info), and errors with full context (error).
- NEVER print — use `self.logger`.
- Test in SANDBOX first. Add at least one unit test under `tests/unit/` and one integration test under `tests/integration/`.
- After implementation, `poetry run python scripts/smoke_import.py` and the public-API contract test MUST pass.

- See `BibliographicRecords.create_representation` / `link_file_to_representation` (`src/almaapitk/domains/bibs.py:569-690`) for binary-upload pattern, but verify whether user attachments uses the same shape before reusing.
- No DELETE endpoint for attachments per the API.

---
Filed as part of the coverage-expansion backlog. See `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-30-coverage-expansion-design.md` for context.
references: Use the `alma-api-expert` skill for endpoint quirks and validation rules
acceptance_criteria: All three endpoints exposed with type hints and Google-style docstrings.
`get_user_attachment` accepts the documented `expand` query parameter
`upload_user_attachment` body shape matches what the developer-network
Unit tests (mocked HTTP) cover: list, get without expand, get with
At least one SANDBOX integration test that round-trips an attachment
No DELETE method is implemented (Alma does not document one).
No `print()` — all operations logged via `alma_logging`. File contents
`scripts/smoke_import.py` and `tests/test_public_api_contract.py` pass.

issues.title: Coverage: Users: fines & fees
issues.body_raw:
**Domain:** Users
**Priority:** High
**Effort:** M (1–3 days)

## Background
Add coverage for the user fines & fees resource. Endpoints and `op` values
match docs, but the audit (2026-05-01) flagged three signature-level
mismatches that need to be corrected before implementation:

1. `pay_all_user_fees` omits the mandatory `op=pay` query param plus the
   literal `amount=ALL` value used by Alma for the bulk-pay endpoint.
2. `dispute_user_fee(... reason: str)` makes `reason` required, but only
   `op=waive` documents `reason` as required — `op=dispute` does not.
3. `method='CASH'` should not be the silent default for non-`pay` ops; it
   only applies when posting `op=pay`.

## Relevant Alma API documentation
- Alma dev-network: https://developers.exlibrisgroup.com/alma/apis/users/
- Endpoints touched:
  - `GET /almaws/v1/users/{user_id}/fees`
  - `POST /almaws/v1/users/{user_id}/fees`
  - `GET /almaws/v1/users/{user_id}/fees/{fee_id}`
  - `POST /almaws/v1/users/{user_id}/fees/all` (bulk pay)
  - `POST /almaws/v1/users/{user_id}/fees/{fee_id}` (`op=pay|waive|dispute|restore`)

Per docs:
- `pay_all` and per-fee `pay` both require `op=pay` plus `amount` (`ALL`,
  `numeric`, etc.) and `method` (`CASH`, `CREDIT_CARD`, `ONLINE`, ...).
- `op=waive` requires `reason`. `op=dispute` and `op=restore` do not.
- All of the above are query parameters, not body fields.

## Current wrapper behavior
None of the fees endpoints are wrapped today.

## Expected wrapper behavior

```python
def list_user_fees(self, user_id: str, status: str = None) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    """Returns the Alma envelope (`total_record_count`, `fee[]`)."""

def create_user_fee(self, user_id: str, fee_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> AlmaResponse: ...

def get_user_fee(self, user_id: str, fee_id: str) -> Dict[str, Any]: ...

def pay_all_user_fees(
    self,
    user_id: str,
    amount: str = 'ALL',         # 'ALL' or numeric string
    method: str = 'CASH',
    external_transaction_id: str = None,
) -> AlmaResponse:
    """POST .../fees/all?op=pay&amount=...&method=..."""

def pay_user_fee(
    self,
    user_id: str,
    fee_id: str,
    amount: str,
    method: str = 'CASH',
    external_transaction_id: str = None,
) -> AlmaResponse:
    """POST .../fees/{fee_id}?op=pay&amount=...&method=..."""

def waive_user_fee(
    self,
    user_id: str,
    fee_id: str,
    reason: str,                 # REQUIRED (op=waive)
    amount: str = None,
    comment: str = None,
) -> AlmaResponse: ...

def dispute_user_fee(
    self,
    user_id: str,
    fee_id: str,
    reason: str = None,          # OPTIONAL (audit-corrected)
    comment: str = None,
) -> AlmaResponse: ...

def restore_user_fee(
    self,
    user_id: str,
    fee_id: str,
    comment: str = None,
) -> AlmaResponse: ...
```

## Implementation notes
- Each action method is a thin wrapper that builds the correct query string
  (`op=...&amount=...&method=...`) and posts to the right URL.
- Keep `method` as a parameter only on payment methods (`pay_all_user_fees`,
  `pay_user_fee`); do NOT make it a default on non-payment ops.
- Validate non-empty `reason` for `waive_user_fee` at call time
  (`AlmaValidationError`).
- Treat `amount` as `str` (not `float`) to avoid floating-point precision
  issues — Alma's API accepts string amounts including the literal `ALL`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `pay_all_user_fees` posts to `.../fees/all` with **`op=pay`** and
      **`amount`** (default `'ALL'`) and **`method`** as query parameters.
- [ ] `pay_user_fee(amount, method)` posts to `.../fees/{fee_id}` with
      `op=pay` plus `amount` and `method` as query params.
- [ ] `waive_user_fee` requires non-empty `reason` (validated) and posts
      `op=waive&reason=...`.
- [ ] `dispute_user_fee.reason` is **optional**; method posts `op=dispute`.
- [ ] `restore_user_fee` posts `op=restore` with no required extras.
- [ ] No method silently injects `method='CASH'` outside payment ops.
- [ ] Unit tests cover each of the 4 `op` values, plus the `pay_all` shape.
- [ ] At least one SANDBOX integration test for each `op`.
- [ ] No `print()` — operations logged via `alma_logging`.
- [ ] `scripts/smoke_import.py` and `tests/test_public_api_contract.py` pass.

## Open questions
- Whether `external_transaction_id` is part of the standard query string for
  online payments — confirm during integration testing.

## Audit notes (2026-05-01)
- Primary audit: **Partially aligned**. `pay_all_user_fees` missed `op=pay`
  + `amount=ALL`; `dispute_user_fee.reason` is wrongly required; `method`
  default leaks into non-pay ops.
- Codex direct audit: **Aligned** (didn't flag the signature-level issues).
- Decision: take the stricter primary-audit recommendation and re-shape the
  signatures so each `op` carries exactly the params Alma documents.

## Files to touch
- `src/almaapitk/domains/users.py` (extend existing `Users` class)
- `tests/unit/domains/test_users.py`

## References
- Alma dev-network: https://developers.exlibrisgroup.com/alma/apis/users/
- Use the `alma-api-expert` skill for endpoint quirks and validation rules — `op` values: `pay`, `waive`, `dispute`, `restore`

<!-- prereqs:auto:begin -->
## Prerequisites

**Recommended (would simplify or improve this work, but not strictly blocking):**
- #3 — Persistent requests.Session (every new HTTP call inherits connection pooling)
- #4 — Consolidate HTTP verbs into _request() (every new method routes through one chokepoint)
- #5 — Retry with exponential backoff for 429 / 5xx (every new method gets 429/5xx resilience for free)
- #9 — Map Alma error codes to specific exception subclasses (new methods raise typed Alma errors instead of generic AlmaAPIError)
- #10 — Propagate tracking_id and alma_code on errors (errors carry tracking_id for support escalation)
- #14 — Replace print() with logger; remove safe_request() (no print() statements in the new methods)
- #11 — iter_paged() generator at the client level (list/search methods should use iter_paged() instead of manual offset loops)

<!-- prereqs:auto:end -->

## Notes for the implementing agent
- Mirror an existing domain method as the pattern source (e.g., `Acquisitions.get_invoice` for read, `Acquisitions.create_invoice_simple` for write).
- All public methods must have type hints + Google-style docstrings.
- Validate required inputs at top of method and raise `AlmaValidationError` with a clear message.
- Log method entry with key parameters (info), success with result identifiers (info), and errors with full context (error).
- NEVER print — use `self.logger`.
- Test in SANDBOX first. Add at least one unit test under `tests/unit/` and one integration test under `tests/integration/`.
- After implementation, `poetry run python scripts/smoke_import.py` and the public-API contract test MUST pass.

- POST /fees/{fee_id} dispatches on `op` — single endpoint, multiple operations.
- `pay_all_user_fees` posts to `/fees/all` (a special endpoint, not a fee_id of "all").
- Always include `method` (CASH / CREDIT_CARD / ONLINE / etc.) for payment ops.

---
Filed as part of the coverage-expansion backlog. See `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-30-coverage-expansion-design.md` for context.
references: Use the `alma-api-expert` skill for endpoint quirks and validation rules — `op` values: `pay`, `waive`, `dispute`, `restore`
acceptance_criteria: `pay_all_user_fees` posts to `.../fees/all` with **`op=pay`** and
`pay_user_fee(amount, method)` posts to `.../fees/{fee_id}` with
`waive_user_fee` requires non-empty `reason` (validated) and posts
`dispute_user_fee.reason` is **optional**; method posts `op=dispute`.
`restore_user_fee` posts `op=restore` with no required extras.
No method silently injects `method='CASH'` outside payment ops.
Unit tests cover each of the 4 `op` values, plus the `pay_all` shape.
At least one SANDBOX integration test for each `op`.
No `print()` — operations logged via `alma_logging`.
`scripts/smoke_import.py` and `tests/test_public_api_contract.py` pass.

issues.title: Coverage: Users: deposits
issues.body_raw:
**Domain:** Users
**Priority:** High
**Effort:** S (≤½ day)

## API endpoints touched
- `GET /almaws/v1/users/{user_id}/deposits`
- `POST /almaws/v1/users/{user_id}/deposits`
- `GET /almaws/v1/users/{user_id}/deposits/{deposit_id}`
- `POST /almaws/v1/users/{user_id}/deposits/{deposit_id}`

## Methods to add
```python
def list_user_deposits(self, user_id: str) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]
def create_user_deposit(self, user_id: str, deposit_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> AlmaResponse
def get_user_deposit(self, user_id: str, deposit_id: str) -> Dict[str, Any]
def perform_user_deposit_action(self, user_id: str, deposit_id: str, op: str) -> AlmaResponse
```

## Files to touch
- `src/almaapitk/domains/users.py (extend existing Users class)`
- `tests/unit/domains/test_users.py`

## References
- Alma dev-network: https://developers.exlibrisgroup.com/alma/apis/users/

<!-- prereqs:auto:begin -->
## Prerequisites

**Recommended (would simplify or improve this work, but not strictly blocking):**
- #3 — Persistent requests.Session (every new HTTP call inherits connection pooling)
- #4 — Consolidate HTTP verbs into _request() (every new method routes through one chokepoint)
- #5 — Retry with exponential backoff for 429 / 5xx (every new method gets 429/5xx resilience for free)
- #9 — Map Alma error codes to specific exception subclasses (new methods raise typed Alma errors instead of generic AlmaAPIError)
- #10 — Propagate tracking_id and alma_code on errors (errors carry tracking_id for support escalation)
- #14 — Replace print() with logger; remove safe_request() (no print() statements in the new methods)
- #11 — iter_paged() generator at the client level (list/search methods should use iter_paged() instead of manual offset loops)

<!-- prereqs:auto:end -->

## Acceptance criteria
- All listed methods implemented on the existing domain class with type hints and docstrings.
- Each method has unit-test coverage (mocked HTTP) and at least one integration test against SANDBOX.
- Errors raise `AlmaValidationError` (input) or `AlmaAPIError` / subclass (API).
- All operations logged through `alma_logging` (no `print`).
- `scripts/smoke_import.py` and `tests/test_public_api_contract.py` still pass.
- If new public symbols are exposed, they are added to `almaapitk/__init__.py` `__all__` and the lazy import map.

## Notes for the implementing agent
- Mirror an existing domain method as the pattern source (e.g., `Acquisitions.get_invoice` for read, `Acquisitions.create_invoice_simple` for write).
- All public methods must have type hints + Google-style docstrings.
- Validate required inputs at top of method and raise `AlmaValidationError` with a clear message.
- Log method entry with key parameters (info), success with result identifiers (info), and errors with full context (error).
- NEVER print — use `self.logger`.
- Test in SANDBOX first. Add at least one unit test under `tests/unit/` and one integration test under `tests/integration/`.
- After implementation, `poetry run python scripts/smoke_import.py` and the public-API contract test MUST pass.

- Deposits are typically used for ILL fees, key deposits, etc. Refer to `alma-api-expert` for action `op` values.

---
Filed as part of the coverage-expansion backlog. See `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-30-coverage-expansion-design.md` for context.
acceptance_criteria: All listed methods implemented on the existing domain class with type hints and docstrings.
Each method has unit-test coverage (mocked HTTP) and at least one integration test against SANDBOX.
Errors raise `AlmaValidationError` (input) or `AlmaAPIError` / subclass (API).
All operations logged through `alma_logging` (no `print`).
`scripts/smoke_import.py` and `tests/test_public_api_contract.py` still pass.
If new public symbols are exposed, they are added to `almaapitk/__init__.py` `__all__` and the lazy import map.
